title: ochrosia alyxioides
intro: Ochrosia alyxioides, also known as the 'Sandpaper Tree' due to its unique bark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, particularly in Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Sandpaper Tree is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
